  "concepts": [
    {"id": "c_drai", "name": "Data Readiness for AI (DRAI)", "aliases": ["DRAI"], "description": "Principles for making data ready for AI, applied here to leadership-scale scientific datasets.", "grounding_span": "Data Readiness for AI (DRAI) principles apply to leadership-scale scientific datasets", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "c_airready", "name": "AI-ready data", "aliases": ["AI-ready datasets", "ready-to-train"], "description": "Data cleaned, labeled, normalized, feature-engineered, and formatted for scalable training.", "grounding_span": "AI-ready data—cleaned, labeled, normalized, feature-engineered, and formatted for scalable training", "location": "§1"},
    {"id": "c_airreadiness", "name": "AI-readiness", "aliases": [], "description": "The degree to which a dataset is prepared for large-scale AI workflows; treated as multi-dimensional rather than binary.", "grounding_span": "the specific challenges of AI-readiness at leadership computing scale", "location": "§1"},
    {"id": "c_foundation_models", "name": "scientific foundation models", "aliases": ["foundation models"], "description": "Large models trained on scientific data, emerging as key enablers of discovery.", "grounding_span": "training scientific foundation models", "location": "§1"},
    {"id": "c_transformer_gen", "name": "transformer-based generative models", "aliases": [], "description": "Generative models increasingly applied across scientific domains, a focus of the readiness framework.", "grounding_span": "transformer-based generative models that are increasingly applied across scientific domains", "location": "§1"},
    {"id": "c_drl", "name": "Data Readiness Levels", "aliases": [], "description": "Five-level axis characterizing degree of technical preparation from raw to fully AI-ready.", "grounding_span": "Data Readiness Levels (raw to AI-ready)", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "c_dps", "name": "Data Processing Stages", "aliases": [], "description": "Axis describing the structural workflow to transform data: ingest to shard.", "grounding_span": "Data Processing Stages (ingest to shard)", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "c_hpc", "name": "high-performance computing (HPC)", "aliases": ["HPC"], "description": "Computing environment to which the readiness framework is tailored.", "grounding_span": "high performance\ncomputing (HPC) environments", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "c_leadership", "name": "leadership-scale computing", "aliases": ["leadership computing facilities"], "description": "Leadership-scale computing facilities such as OLCF where scientific datasets are prepared.", "grounding_span": "leadership-scale scientific datasets", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "c_preprocessing", "name": "data preprocessing", "aliases": ["preprocessing"], "description": "Transformation of raw scientific data into formats suitable for large-scale training.", "grounding_span": "common preprocessing patterns", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "c_data_cleaning", "name": "data cleaning", "aliases": [], "description": "Cleaning step in the common preprocessing sequence.", "grounding_span": "a common\nsequence of data cleaning, normalization, labeling, feature engineering, and format conversion", "location": "§2.1"},
    {"id": "c_normalization", "name": "normalization", "aliases": [], "description": "Normalizing data, e.g. by mean and standard deviation.", "grounding_span": "normalizing\nby mean and standard deviation", "location": "§2.1"},
    {"id": "c_labeling", "name": "labeling", "aliases": [], "description": "Manual or automated labeling of datasets.", "grounding_span": "Some datasets require manual or automated labeling", "location": "§2.1"},
    {"id": "c_feature_engineering", "name": "feature engineering", "aliases": [], "description": "Selecting the most informative set or combination of features to train on.", "grounding_span": "feature engineering to select the most\ninformative set of features or combination of features on which to train", "location": "§2.1"},
    {"id": "c_format_conversion", "name": "format conversion", "aliases": [], "description": "Converting data into standard compressed and sharded formats.", "grounding_span": "exported in a standard compressed and sharded format", "location": "§2.1"},
    {"id": "c_sharding", "name": "sharding", "aliases": [], "description": "Partitioning data into shards in binary formats for scalable, high-performance I/O.", "grounding_span": "sharding for high-performance\nI/O", "location": "§3.5"},
    {"id": "c_data_augmentation", "name": "data augmentation", "aliases": [], "description": "Techniques to increase sample count, e.g. rotating images, adding noise, synthetic samples.", "grounding_span": "certain data\naugmentation techniques may be employed to increase the number of samples", "location": "§2.1"},
    {"id": "c_semi_supervised", "name": "semi-supervised learning", "aliases": [], "description": "Methods leveraging both labeled and unlabeled samples.", "grounding_span": "semi-supervised learning methods can leverage both labeled and unlabeled samples", "location": "§2.1"},
    {"id": "c_pseudo_labeling", "name": "pseudo-labeling", "aliases": [], "description": "Iteratively treating model predictions on unlabeled data as labels.", "grounding_span": "pseudo-labeling, where model predictions on unlabeled data are iteratively treated as labels\nto improve training", "location": "§2.1"},
    {"id": "c_train_test_split", "name": "train/test/validation split", "aliases": [], "description": "Splitting data into train, test, and validation sets before export.", "grounding_span": "the data should be split into train, test,\nand validation sets", "location": "§2.1"},
    {"id": "c_binary_formats", "name": "binary formats", "aliases": [], "description": "Binary storage formats used for AI-ready sharded data.", "grounding_span": "binary formats such as HDF5, ADIOS [25], or TFRecords", "location": "§2.1"},
    {"id": "c_parallel_io", "name": "parallel file I/O", "aliases": [], "description": "High-throughput parallel I/O required for efficient training at scale.", "grounding_span": "high-throughput,\nparallel file I/O", "location": "§1"},
    {"id": "c_climate", "name": "climate domain", "aliases": ["climate science"], "description": "One of four strategic scientific domains surveyed for AI-readiness.", "grounding_span": "four strategic domains—climate, fusion, bio/health, and materials science", "location": "§1"},
    {"id": "c_fusion", "name": "fusion domain", "aliases": ["nuclear fusion", "fusion research"], "description": "Fusion research domain leveraging simulation and experimental plasma data.", "grounding_span": "Fusion research leverages both simulation and experimental data to model plasma behavior", "location": "§3.2"},
    {"id": "c_biohealth", "name": "bio/health domain", "aliases": ["bio/health"], "description": "Domain spanning molecular modeling, clinical imaging, and genomic sequence prediction.", "grounding_span": "AI workflows in bio/health span molecular modeling, clinical imaging, and genomic sequence prediction", "location": "§3.3"},
    {"id": "c_materials", "name": "materials science domain", "aliases": ["materials"], "description": "Domain relying on DFT calculations and graph-based models of atomic structures.", "grounding_span": "Materials datasets frequently stem from density functional theory (DFT) calculations", "location": "§3.4"},
    {"id": "c_gnn", "name": "graph neural networks (GNNs)", "aliases": ["GNN"], "description": "Models representing atomic structures, bonding, and electronic properties as graphs.", "grounding_span": "training graph neural networks (GNNs)", "location": "§3.4"},
    {"id": "c_regridding", "name": "regridding / spatiotemporal alignment", "aliases": ["regridding"], "description": "Domain-specific transformation aligning/resampling grids, notably in climate.", "grounding_span": "regridding in climate science", "location": "§4"},
    {"id": "c_anonymization", "name": "anonymization", "aliases": [], "description": "Privacy-preserving transformation, notably in health data.", "grounding_span": "anonymization in health data", "location": "§4"},
    {"id": "c_graph_encoding", "name": "graph encoding", "aliases": [], "description": "Encoding atomic structures as graph representations, notably in materials science.", "grounding_span": "graph encoding in materials science", "location": "§4"},
    {"id": "c_precision", "name": "floating-point precision", "aliases": ["32-bit or 64-bit precision"], "description": "High-precision numerics needed to preserve numerical stability and physical realism.", "grounding_span": "32-bit or 64-bit floating-point precision to\npreserve numerical stability and physical realism", "location": "§2.2"},
    {"id": "c_physical_constraints", "name": "physical constraints", "aliases": ["conservation laws", "boundary conditions"], "description": "Domain-specific physics constraints that models must adhere to.", "grounding_span": "domain-specific constraints such as conservation\nlaws and boundary conditions", "location": "§2.2"},
    {"id": "c_provenance", "name": "provenance", "aliases": [], "description": "Traceable links between raw data, preprocessing steps, and trained models.", "grounding_span": "provenance capture remains ad hoc", "location": "§5"},
    {"id": "c_reproducibility", "name": "reproducibility", "aliases": [], "description": "Ability to reproduce workflows, essential in open science settings.", "grounding_span": "Provenance and Reproducibility.", "location": "§5"},
    {"id": "c_secure_enclaves", "name": "secure enclaves", "aliases": [], "description": "Secure computing environments motivated by sensitive data such as PHI/PII.", "grounding_span": "use of secure enclaves and federated learning strategies", "location": "§3.3"},
    {"id": "c_federated_learning", "name": "federated learning", "aliases": [], "description": "Strategy for training on distributed sensitive data.", "grounding_span": "use of secure enclaves and federated learning strategies", "location": "§3.3"},
    {"id": "c_phi", "name": "protected health information (PHI)", "aliases": ["PHI"], "description": "Sensitive health information requiring compliance controls.", "grounding_span": "protected health information (PHI) and personally identifiable information (PII)", "location": "§3.3"},
    {"id": "c_pii", "name": "personally identifiable information (PII)", "aliases": ["PII"], "description": "Personal data requiring compliance controls.", "grounding_span": "protected health information (PHI) and personally identifiable information (PII)", "location": "§3.3"},
    {"id": "c_cui", "name": "controlled unclassified information (CUI)", "aliases": ["CUI"], "description": "Sensitive national-security-related data requiring secure handling.", "grounding_span": "often contain PHI, PII, or CUI", "location": "§5"},
    {"id": "c_data_scarcity", "name": "data scarcity", "aliases": ["quality variability"], "description": "Under-sampling, imbalance, and inconsistency limiting ML training.", "grounding_span": "Data Scarcity and Quality Variability.", "location": "§5"},
    {"id": "c_fragmentation", "name": "fragmentation across domains", "aliases": [], "description": "Inconsistent formats, terminology, and documentation across scientific communities.", "grounding_span": "Fragmentation Across Domains.", "location": "§5"},
    {"id": "c_one_hot", "name": "one-hot encoding", "aliases": [], "description": "Encoding used, e.g., for DNA sequences in Enformer.", "grounding_span": "Enformer transforms DNA sequences via one-hot encoding", "location": "§3.3"},
    {"id": "c_msa", "name": "multiple sequence alignment (MSA)", "aliases": ["MSA"], "description": "Preprocessing step in AlphaFold 2's pipeline.", "grounding_span": "multiple sequence alignment (MSA)", "location": "§3.3"},
    {"id": "c_feature_extraction", "name": "domain-specific feature extraction", "aliases": [], "description": "Domain-informed extraction of features as part of the readiness workflow.", "grounding_span": "domain-informed feature extraction", "location": "§3.5"},
    {"id": "c_stage_ingest", "name": "ingest", "aliases": [], "description": "Data processing stage: acquisition and validated ingestion into standard formats.", "grounding_span": "ingest → preprocess → transform → structure → shard", "location": "§3.5"},
    {"id": "c_stage_preprocess", "name": "preprocess", "aliases": [], "description": "Data processing stage broadly shared across domains.", "grounding_span": "ingest → preprocess → transform → structure → shard", "location": "§3.5"},
    {"id": "c_stage_transform", "name": "transform", "aliases": [], "description": "Stage capturing domain-specific conversions such as regridding or anonymization.", "grounding_span": "ingest → preprocess → transform → structure → shard", "location": "§3.5"},
    {"id": "c_stage_structure", "name": "structure", "aliases": [], "description": "Stage organizing data into standardized formats such as tensors, time-series, or graphs.", "grounding_span": "ingest → preprocess → transform → structure → shard", "location": "§3.5"},
    {"id": "c_stage_shard", "name": "shard", "aliases": [], "description": "Stage partitioning and sharding data into binary formats for scalable ingestion.", "grounding_span": "ingest → preprocess → transform → structure → shard", "location": "§3.5"},
    {"id": "c_lvl_raw", "name": "raw (readiness level 1)", "aliases": [], "description": "Level 1: initial raw acquisition.", "grounding_span": "1 - Raw", "location": "Table 2"},
    {"id": "c_lvl_cleaned", "name": "cleaned (readiness level 2)", "aliases": [], "description": "Level 2: validated ingestion and initial alignment/regridding.", "grounding_span": "2 - Cleaned", "location": "Table 2"},
    {"id": "c_lvl_labeled", "name": "labeled (readiness level 3)", "aliases": [], "description": "Level 3: metadata enrichment, standardized grids, basic labels added.", "grounding_span": "3 - Labeled", "location": "Table 2"},
    {"id": "c_lvl_feature", "name": "feature-engineered (readiness level 4)", "aliases": [], "description": "Level 4: finalized normalization/anonymization, comprehensive labeling, feature extraction.", "grounding_span": "4 - Feature-engineered", "location": "Table 2"},
    {"id": "c_lvl_ready", "name": "fully AI-ready (readiness level 5)", "aliases": [], "description": "Level 5: automated pipelines, data partitioned into train/test/val and sharded into binary formats.", "grounding_span": "5 - Fully AI-ready", "location": "Table 2"}
  ],
  "definitions": [
    {"id": "d_airready", "term": "AI-ready data", "verbatim_text": "AI-ready data—cleaned, labeled, normalized, feature-engineered, and formatted for scalable training", "normative_status": "academic", "as_of_date": "2025", "grounding_span": "AI-ready data—cleaned, labeled, normalized, feature-engineered, and formatted for scalable training", "location": "§1"},
    {"id": "d_airready_state", "term": "AI-ready state", "verbatim_text": "an AI-ready state—defined by\nsharded storage in binary formats such as HDF5, ADIOS [25], or TFRecords", "normative_status": "academic", "as_of_date": "2025", "grounding_span": "an AI-ready state—defined by\nsharded storage in binary formats such as HDF5, ADIOS [25], or TFRecords", "location": "§2.1"},
    {"id": "d_pseudo", "term": "pseudo-labeling", "verbatim_text": "pseudo-labeling, where model predictions on unlabeled data are iteratively treated as labels\nto improve training", "normative_status": "academic", "as_of_date": "2025", "grounding_span": "pseudo-labeling, where model predictions on unlabeled data are iteratively treated as labels\nto improve training", "location": "§2.1"},
    {"id": "d_transform", "term": "transform stage", "verbatim_text": "Thetransform stage captures domain-specific conversions (e.g., regridding in climate or anonymization\nin health)", "normative_status": "academic", "as_of_date": "2025", "grounding_span": "Thetransform stage captures domain-specific conversions (e.g., regridding in climate or anonymization", "location": "§3.5"},
    {"id": "d_structure", "term": "structure stage", "verbatim_text": "structure refers to organizing the data into standardized formats, such as fixed tensor layouts, hierarchical\ntime-series, or graphs", "normative_status": "academic", "as_of_date": "2025", "grounding_span": "structure refers to organizing the data into standardized formats, such as fixed tensor layouts, hierarchical\ntime-series, or graphs", "location": "§3.5"}
  ],
  "claims": [
    {"id": "cl1", "claim_text": "The performance of AI systems is fundamentally limited by the quality and readiness of the data they consume.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "The performance of AI systems is fundamentally limited by the quality and readiness of the data they consume.", "location": "§1"},
    {"id": "cl2", "claim_text": "Transforming domain-specific datasets into AI-ready formats remains labor-intensive and fragmented.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "transforming domain-specific datasets into AI-ready\nformats remains labor-intensive and fragmented", "location": "§1"},
    {"id": "cl3", "claim_text": "The ClimaX model was trained on over ten terabytes of climate data.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "The ClimaX model, for\nexample, was trained on over ten terabytes of climate data [28].", "location": "§1"},
    {"id": "cl4", "claim_text": "Fusion scientists spend upwards of 70% of their time on data curation.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "scientists spend upwards of 70% of their time on data curation", "location": "§3.2"},
    {"id": "cl5", "claim_text": "OMat24 offers over 100 million labeled DFT-derived structures.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "OMat24 offers over 100 million", "location": "§3.4"},
    {"id": "cl6", "claim_text": "AI-readiness is not a binary state but a spectrum shaped by domain constraints, data heterogeneity, and platform requirements.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "AI-readiness is not a binary state, but a spectrum shaped by domain constraints", "location": "§3.5"},
    {"id": "cl7", "claim_text": "Engineering and physics-based models often demand 32-bit or 64-bit floating-point precision to preserve numerical stability and physical realism.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "engineering and physics-based models often demand 32-bit or 64-bit floating-point precision to\npreserve numerical stability and physical realism", "location": "§2.2"},
    {"id": "cl8", "claim_text": "Scientific AI poses distinct challenges compared to commercial AI.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "Scientific AI poses distinct challenges compared to commercial AI.", "location": "§1"},
    {"id": "cl9", "claim_text": "Fusion scientists spend the majority of their time curating data due to inconsistent formats and missing labels.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "fusion scientists spend the majority of their time curating data", "location": "§2.3"},
    {"id": "cl10", "claim_text": "Provenance capture in HPC-scale scientific AI workflows remains ad hoc.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "provenance capture remains ad hoc", "location": "§5"}
  ],

    {"id": "s_imas", "name": "IMAS (Integrated Modeling and Analysis Suite)", "version": null, "steward": "ITER", "as_of_date": "2025", "grounding_span": "Integrated Modeling and Analysis Suite (IMAS)", "location": "§3.2"},

  "proposed_relationships": [
    {"grounding_span": "A common preprocessing pattern across these deep learning models is the\nsequence: download → regrid → normalize → shard.", "suggested_edge": "has_domain_workflow_pattern", "from_hint": "c_climate", "to_hint": "download → regrid → normalize → shard", "note": "Domain-to-workflow-pattern mapping; schema lacks a workflow-pattern node/edge."},
    {"grounding_span": "we observe a consistent pattern:\nextract → align → normalize → shard.", "suggested_edge": "has_domain_workflow_pattern", "from_hint": "c_fusion", "to_hint": "extract → align → normalize → shard", "note": "Domain-to-workflow-pattern mapping."},
    {"grounding_span": "exhibit a consistent pipeline: parse → normalize", "suggested_edge": "has_domain_workflow_pattern", "from_hint": "c_materials", "to_hint": "parse → normalize → encode → shard", "note": "Domain-to-workflow-pattern mapping."},
    {"grounding_span": "METRIC [ 33] for\nmedical data or NOAA’s climate data maturity model [5]", "suggested_edge": "applies_to_domain", "from_hint": "f_metric", "to_hint": "medical data", "note": "Maturity framework scoped to a domain; no domain-scoping edge in schema."},
    {"grounding_span": "OpenAI’s ChatGPT was used to provide editing suggestions for several sentences\nin the paper, as well as to help analyze some of the preprocessing patterns", "suggested_edge": "authoring_tool_used", "from_hint": "data-readiness-for-scientific-ai-at-scale", "to_hint": "OpenAI ChatGPT", "note": "Tool-use disclosure for authoring; not expressible with allowed edges."},
    {"grounding_span": "This pipeline is inherently\niterative: data preparation outcomes inform subsequent model training, and model performance provides feedback that\ntriggers further data refinement and augmentation.", "suggested_edge": "feedback_loop_between", "from_hint": "c_preprocessing", "to_hint": "model training", "note": "Iterative feedback relationship between preprocessing and model training."}
  ]
